stem: A telecommunications communication network uses 3 parallel relays for redundancy. The message delivered requires at least one relay to function. relay 1 has a reliability of 78%. relay 2 has a reliability of 86%. relay 3 has a reliability between 73% and 84% (uncertain due to limited operational data). What is the overall probability that the message delivered?

ground_truth.answer: P(message delivered) ranges from 0.9917 to 0.9951. Point estimate: 0.9932.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 relays fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.22 × 0.14 × 0.27 = 0.0083. P(works) = 0.9917.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.22 × 0.14 × 0.16 = 0.0049. P(works) = 0.9951.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
